_comment: Ground truth annotation — GENOMICS checklist for Rolain et al. 2009 genomic study

article.title: Genomic analysis of an emerging multiresistant Staphylococcus aureus strain rapidly spreading in cystic fibrosis patients revealed the presence of an antibiotic inducible bacteriophage
article.authors: Rolain JM, François P, Hernandez D, Bittar F, Richet H, et al.

study_type.notes: Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis of MRSA strain (CF-Marseille) from cystic fibrosis patients. Includes 454 pyrosequencing, microarray, molecular genotyping, and epidemiological characterization.

1.notes: Title clearly identifies as genomic analysis: 'Genomic analysis of an emerging multiresistant Staphylococcus aureus strain'

2.notes: Background: S. aureus as major pathogen; MRSA prevalence increasing; CF airways as model ecosystem for resistant bacteria emergence; antibiotic selection pressure in CF

3.notes: Sample collection: 2006 in Marseille, France; epidemiological survey of S. aureus in CF patients; detection of atypical MRSA strain (CF-Marseille)

4.notes: Sample sequenced: one representative isolate (CF-Marseille); retrospective epidemiological analysis of S. aureus isolated 2002-2007 in CF patients at institution

5.notes: Sequencing platform: high-throughput sequencing system (454 Life Science Corp., Roche); yielded 19,064,566 nucleotides; 131 assembled contigs (546-210,978 bp); total 2.83 Mbp

6.notes: Library preparation: 454 pyrosequencing coupled with microarrays and molecular genotyping methods; extraction and amplification protocols implicit in platform description

7.notes: Quality control specific to raw sequencing reads not explicitly detailed (Q30, adapter removal not mentioned); focus on assembly and contigs

8.notes: Assembly method: 131 contigs assembled; total length 2.83 Mbp; reference-based comparison to other S. aureus genomes (N315, MW2, USA300, Mu50, NCTC 8325, COL, MRSA252)

9.notes: Bioinformatics pipeline: Glimmer software for CDS prediction; UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot database for protein matching; protein identity threshold of ≥90%; comparison approach described

10.notes: Phylogenetic analysis: Complete Genome Hybridization (CGH) and Multi Locus VNTR Assay performed; phylogenetic relationships to Mu50, USA300 identified; closest match USA300/Mu50 with 2522 matches (95% of proteome)

11.notes: Statistical analysis: parsimony analysis; recurrent mutations at specific positions noted; gap coding as single deletion events; comparison of gene expression profiles between strains

12.notes: Minimal clinical metadata; study focused on genomic characterization rather than clinical correlations; CF status and antibiotic exposure history not formally linked

13.notes: No sequencing quality metrics (depth, coverage) reported; assembly only summarized by contig size range and total length

14.notes: Sequence characteristics: total 2.83 Mbp; 2736 hypothetical proteins predicted; 2675 (97.8%) matched known proteins at ≥90% identity; core genome similar to other MRSA but different genomic organization

15.notes: Genetic diversity reported: 61 proteins unique to CF-Marseille; novel SCCmec type IV mosaic cassette identified; new phage (44 Kb) closely related to phiETA3; resistance genes identified (methicillin, fluoroquinolone)

16.notes: Phylogenetic results: Complete Genome Hybridization showing relationship to Mu50 (vancomycin-resistant S. aureus); phylogenetic analysis suggests polyclonal MRSA populations in CF patients

17.notes: Drug resistance mutations: chromosomally-encoded beta-lactam and ofloxacin resistance identified; hetero-GISA (Glycopeptide-Intermediate) phenotype; antibiotic inducible phage mobilization described

18.notes: Epidemiological interpretation: emergence and spread of well-adapted MRSA in CF niche; antibiotic selection pressure driving evolution; phage induction and lateral gene transfer mechanisms; public health implications for CF population

19.notes: Data availability: 131 contigs available in EMBL Nucleotide Sequence Database, accession number DS:71627; supplementary files referenced (Additional files 1-2)

20.notes: Analysis code/methods: Glimmer software, CGH analysis, MLVA genotyping described; specific methods and parameters provided for reproducibility

21.notes: No formal ethics statement; strain from routine CF patient cultures; no patient consent issues (genomic sequencing of established isolate)

22.notes: No funding source explicitly stated; no competing interests declared for authors
